steps.text: Identify the like terms. Both \(12ab\) and \(6ab\) share the same variable factor \(ab\), so they can be combined by subtracting their coefficients: \(12ab - 6ab = (12-6)ab = 6ab\).

Evaluate each choice:
  • Choice A: 2 — a plain number with no variables. \(12ab-6ab\) retains the variable factor \(ab\). Incorrect.
  • Choice B: 6 — also a plain number with no variables. Incorrect.
  • Choice C: \(ab\) — coefficient 1, but the correct coefficient is 6. Incorrect.
  • Choice D: \(6ab\) — matches \((12-6)ab\) exactly. That matches choice D — D is correct.
